description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from dark, roughly hewn stone blocks, giving it a rugged and ancient appearance. The archway is supported by two thick, cylindrical pillars, each composed of irregularly shaped stones stacked atop one another. The arch itself is a semi-circular structure, with the stones arranged in a keystone pattern at the apex. There are no visible decorative reliefs, carvings, or inscription panels, emphasizing a utilitarian and robust design. The surface texture is coarse and uneven, suggesting weathering and age. The overall form is simple and monumental, reminiscent of early Roman or medieval stonework.
description: A dark, rugged stone triumphal arch with thick, cylindrical pillars and a semi-circular keystone arch. The surface is coarse and weathered, with no visible decorations.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full archway and both cylindrical stone pillars clearly.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the narrow profile and thickness of the arch structure.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the archway and pillars from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the narrow profile and stone texture.
